final_embedding_text:
Context: This document is a MarketBeat report by Cushman & Wakefield, focusing on the industrial and warehousing sector in Bengaluru, India. It provides an overview of market fundamentals, leasing volumes, supply, rentals, and land rates for H1 2025, with comparisons to H2 2024 and H1 2024.

Chunk Content: # MARKETBEAT BENGALURU INDUSTRIAL H1 2025

## MARKET FUNDAMENTALS

| MARKET FUNDAMENTALS | H2 2024 | H1 2025 |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Inventory (in MSF) | 45 | 47 |
| Vacancy (%) | 20 | 15 |
| Upcoming Supply in next 12 months (MSF) | 5.5 | 3.5 |
| L&I Leasing (in MSF) | 2.0 | 3.3 |
| Average Rentals Range (INR PSFPM) | 22.5 | 22.5 |
| Land Transacted (in acres) | 355 | 150 |
| Land Values Range (INR MN per acre) | 25-35 | 25-35 |

## SLOWER WAREHOUSE LEASE VOLUMES IN H1
Bengaluru recorded 1.7 msf of warehousing lease volumes in H1 2025, lower than the exceptionally strong activity seen in H1 2024 and around 5% lower than H2 2024. The western corridor dominated warehouse leasing activity, accounting for over 50% of lease volumes in H1 with locations such as Nelamangala, Dabaspet and Bidadi the major contributors. The southern corridor followed with a 30% share, driven by leasing activity in Jigani, Kumbalgodu, and Begur. East Bengaluru accounted for the remaining 19% of lease volumes with space takeup recorded at Hoskote. E-commerce firms contributed around 46% of warehouse lease volumes in H1 followed by manufacturing (27%). Retail segment accounted for 10% of the lease volumes. The contribution of 3PL operators, which have been dominating space takeup in previous quarters, contributed just 6% of lease volumes in H1.

## HEALTHY SUPPLY ADDITION H1
Bengaluru recorded warehouse supply addition of around 2 msf in H1 with the city’s total inventory (A+B) at 47 msf at the end of the period. Supply pipeline remains strong with leading developers and warehouse operators moving ahead with projects to cater to strong demand. A number of built-to-suit (BTS) facilities are also coming up. Land parcels have also been transacted across prominent locations at the southern, western and eastern submarkets. Land prices rose by 9-10% yoy.

## INDUSTRIAL LEASE VOLUME SURGES BY 2.5X ON ANNUAL BASIS
Bengaluru recorded industrial leasing of 1.6 msf in H1, a jump of over 2.5x over the same period last year. Industrial leasing was recorded by energy and aerospace firms at north submarket (Doddaballapur, Devanahalli), which had a share of 35%. South Bengaluru (Anekal, Bommasandra) contributed 28% of lease volumes. Headline industrial rentals increased by 3-4% on an annual basis in H1.

### INDUSTRIAL RENT / Y-O-Y GROWTH
| Submarket | Industrial Rent (INR/SF/MO) | Industrial Rent Y-O-Y % Growth |
| :--- | :---: | :---: |
| Bommasandra | ~31.5 | ~3.5% |
| Peenya &... | ~43.5 | ~3.5% |
| Kumbalgodu | ~24.0 | ~2.5% |
| Narsapur | ~25.0 | ~3.5% |
| Doddaballapur | ~23.0 | ~3.5% |

**Caption:** INDUSTRIAL RENT / Y-O-Y GROWTH. Left Axis: Industrial Rent (INR PSFPM); Right Axis: Industrial Rent Y-O-Y % Growth.

### WAREHOUSING RENT / Y-O-Y GROWTH
| Submarket | Warehousing Rent (INR/SF/MO) | Warehousing Rent Y-O-Y % Growth |
| :--- | :---: | :---: |
| Hosur Road (Jigani...) | ~25.5 | ~3.5% |
| Nelamangala | ~23.5 | ~4.5% |
| Bidadi | ~27.5 | ~3.5% |
| Hoskote | ~24.5 | ~4.5% |
| Devanahalli/KIADB... | ~32.5 | ~4.5% |

**Caption:** WAREHOUSING RENT / Y-O-Y GROWTH. Left Axis: Warehousing Rent (INR PSFPM); Right Axis: Warehousing Rent Y-O-Y % Growth.

Context: This section from the Cushman & Wakefield "BENGALURU INDUSTRIAL H1 2025" report provides detailed insights into the industrial and warehousing real estate market in Bengaluru. It presents key submarket rentals and land rates for June 2025, alongside significant industrial/warehouse and land transactions recorded during H1 2025.

Chunk Content: # BENGALURU INDUSTRIAL H1 2025

### KEY WAREHOUSING SUBMARKETS – RENTALS AND LAND RATES – JUNE 2025
| SUBMARKET | WAREHOUSE RENT (INR/SF/MONTH) | WAREHOUSE RENT (Y-O-Y CHANGE) | LAND RATES (INR MN/ACRE) | LAND RATES (US$ MN/ACRE) | LAND RATES (EURO MN/ACRE) | LAND RATES (Y-O-Y CHANGE) |
| :--- | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: |
| Hosur Road (Jigani, Attibele) | 24-27 | 3-4% | 70-120 | 0.82-1.41 | 0.73-1.24 | 9-10% |
| Nelamangala | 22-25 | 4-5% | 35-50 | 0.41-0.59 | 0.36-0.52 | 9-10% |
| Bidadii | 25-30 | 3-4% | 55-65 | 0.64-0.76 | 0.57-0.67 | 7-8% |
| Hoskote, Malur | 23-26 | 4-5% | 35-40 | 0.41-0.47 | 0.36-0.41 | 9-10% |
| Devanahalli/KIADB Hitech Zone | 30-35 | 4-5% | 60-100 | 0.70-1.17 | 0.62-1.04 | 8-9% |

### KEY INDUSTRIAL SUBMARKETS – RENTALS AND LAND RATES – JUNE 2025
| SUBMARKET | INDUSTRIAL RENT (INR/SF/MONTH) | INDUSTRIAL RENT (Y-O-Y CHANGE) | LAND RATES (INR MN/ACRE) | LAND RATES (US$ MN/ACRE) | LAND RATES (EURO MN/ACRE) | LAND RATES (Y-O-Y CHANGE) |
| :--- | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: |
| Bommasandra | 30-33 | 3-4% | 175-195 | 2.05-2.28 | 1.82-2.02 | 8-10% |
| Peenya | 42-45 | 3-4% | 220-260 | 2.58-3.04 | 2.28-2.70 | 6-8% |
| Kumbalgodu | 23-25 | 2-3% | 110-130 | 1.29-1.52 | 1.14-1.35 | 6-8% |
| Narsapura | 23-27 | 3-4% | 25-30 | 0.29-0.35 | 0.26-0.31 | 8-10% |
| Doddaballapur | 21-25 | 3-4% | 25-35 | 0.29-0.41 | 0.26-0.36 | 8-10% |

Note: Quoted industrial and warehousing rents are historically corrected to reflect accurate market conditions.
Quoted land rates for serviced industrial land parcels are mentioned
# Represents government owned serviced plots in industrial parks
Percentage growth are in local currency; Y-O-Y – Year on Year
Conversion Rate: US$1= 85.4 INR and Euro 1 = INR 96.4
